title: Trump’s Arrival in Turkey for NATO Summit Adds Drama and Spectacle
summary: The second President Trump landed in Turkey, the center of gravity shifted right to where he likes it best: himself.

title: Oil Prices Jump as U.S. and Iran Resume Strikes, and Big Tobacco Makes a Big New Bet
summary: Plus, using drones to watch for sharks.

title: Global Economic Output Looks Slower for 2026, IMF Says
summary: The I.M.F. projected world output would fall to 3 percent for the year, a number pushed down by high commodity prices.

title: As Israel Loses Support in the U.S., Rahm Emanuel Criticizes Netanyahu
summary: The former Chicago mayor, a Democrat exploring a 2028 presidential run, is in Tel Aviv calling for an end to unconditional U.S. support of Israel.

title: Midtown Neighborhood Has to Cope in a Troubled Building’s Shadow
summary: Travelers were shut out of major hotels, and work ground to a halt for some on Tuesday after a building partially buckled in Manhattan.

title: The Low-Key Lawyer at the Center of the Search for a Platner Replacement
summary: Charles Dingman, chair of the Maine Democratic Party and a progressive, would play a key role in choosing the state’s Democratic Senate candidate if Graham Platner leaves the race.

title: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race
summary: Mr. Platner has not yet withdrawn from the race after a rape allegation, but Democrats in the state and nationally are already exploring potential alternatives.

title: The Unknown Universe Inside Your Gut
summary: The human microbiome is essential to our health, but scientists have only begun to understand why. Two researchers set out to map this largely uncharted terrain.

title: The Alluring Albanian Island Inspiring Ivanka’s Fantasy
summary: A former military base in the Adriatic Sea, the island is in a beautiful setting but it is strewed with snakes, crumbling buildings and land mines.

title: New Rules Require Election Changes for States to Get Terrorism Grants
summary: Federal officials said they would withhold some money unless states pursue paper ballot systems, verify citizenship and conduct costly audits.

title: Justice Department Threatens Top Election Officials Over Noncitizen Voting
summary: The letters come amid President Trump’s effort to tighten election rules to prevent a problem that doesn’t exist: widespread noncitizen voting.

title: Judge Quashes Justice Dept. Subpoena in 2020 Election Inquiry
summary: The ruling cast serious doubts on the department’s efforts to reinvestigate the race, finding that it was too late to bring criminal charges in the case.

title: At Iran’s Funeral for Ayatollah Khamenei, Mourners Bid an Emotional Farewell
summary: Millions of Iranians descended on Tehran to pay their respects to the slain supreme leader.

title: Iraqis Turn Out in Droves to Mourn Khamenei in Show of Solidarity
summary: In the cities of Najaf and Karbala, home to two of Shiite Islam’s holiest sites, hundreds of thousands attended commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who extended Iran’s influence in Iraq.

title: Marine Le Pen’s Return Has Sidelined Her Protégé. Can They Join Forces?
summary: For months, Jordan Bardella was set to be the French far-right’s candidate for the presidency — until his mentor, Marine Le Pen, nudged him aside this week. Their relationship could define the campaign.

title: Are Omega-3 Supplements Actually Good for Your Brain?
summary: Researchers have been surprised by the study findings.

title: As Parents Reject Vitamin K Shots, Some Babies Develop Devastating Bleeding
summary: Doctors described treating brain and abdominal hemorrhages in infants who hadn’t received the routine injection. Several said the images of those patients were seared in their minds.

title: How Revolutionary Was the American Revolution?
summary: Many historians have recently seen it as a tame, even disappointing affair. But in the Trump era, the old question of its radicalism is taking on a fresh charge.

title: This Is One of the Hardest Jobs in America. Millions Are Doing It Alone.
summary: Behind closed doors, millions of Americans are stepping into one of the hardest roles they’ll ever take on: caring for their aging parents.

title: A Cease-Fire in Doubt
summary: We have the latest on renewed strikes in the Middle East.

title: Late Night Calls a Foul on Trump After His World Cup Intervention
summary: President Trump may not have known what a red card in soccer is, Ronny Chieng said: “But you know what he did know? How to get a guy a pardon.”

title: For Rebels in Congo, Ebola Could Help Strengthen Their Rule
summary: A violent militia that controls large swaths of Congolese territory in the east may use the virus to try to bolster its authority.
